metadata.purpose: Documentation taxonomy and concept registry for XAF knowledge management

concepts.name: XAF Application
concepts.description: The root application object that bootstraps and hosts an XAF application, wires together modules, platforms, and services.

concepts.name: Modules
concepts.description: Reusable, composable units that extend XAF application functionality. Modules package business objects, controllers, and model extensions into independently installable components.

concepts.name: Application Model
concepts.description: Metadata-driven configuration system that defines XAF application UI structure, view layouts, captions, and behavior. Stored in XAFML files and mergeable at runtime.

concepts.name: Application Model Extension
concepts.description: Techniques for reading, customizing, and extending the Application Model programmatically using model nodes, node generators, and custom interfaces.

concepts.name: Model Differences
concepts.description: Mechanism for storing and merging incremental Application Model customizations made at runtime or design time into persistent difference storages.

concepts.name: Model Cache
concepts.description: Application Model caching system that pre-compiles and stores the merged model to reduce application startup time.

concepts.name: Dependency Injection
concepts.description: The .NET dependency injection (DI) container integration in XAF that allows registering and resolving services in controllers, business objects, and modules.

concepts.name: Multi-Tenancy
concepts.description: XAF's built-in SaaS multi-tenancy support that isolates data, configuration, and users across multiple tenants within a single application deployment.

concepts.name: Tenant Classes and Resolver
concepts.description: Customization of the tenant entity class, tenant resolver (URL-based, ID-based), tenant login selection, and in-app tenant switching.

concepts.name: AI Integration
concepts.description: Integration of AI capabilities (LLM-based assistance, AI extensions) into XAF applications, including AI-powered UI features and backend AI service connectors.

concepts.name: Enterprise Development Features
concepts.description: Set of XAF capabilities suited for large-scale enterprise applications, including middleware tiers, advanced security, multi-tenancy, scalable deployment, and compliance features.

concepts.name: Security System
concepts.description: XAF's integrated security framework providing authentication, authorization, permission management, and data-level access control for XAF applications.

concepts.name: Authentication
concepts.description: Mechanisms for verifying user identity in XAF, including standard logon forms, custom authentication strategies, OAuth/OpenID providers, and Windows Authentication.

concepts.name: OAuth and Identity Providers
concepts.description: Integration with external OAuth 2.0 and OpenID Connect identity providers (Microsoft Entra ID, Google, GitHub) for XAF application authentication.

concepts.name: Authorization and Permissions
concepts.description: Role-based and policy-based access control in XAF, covering object-level, member-level, and action permissions, including built-in permission setup and custom permission implementations.

concepts.name: Users and Roles
concepts.description: User and role entities in XAF security, including built-in PermissionPolicyUser and PermissionPolicyRole, and guidance on implementing custom user and role classes.

concepts.name: Middle-Tier Application Server
concepts.description: XAF's middleware application server architecture that separates the client tier from the data tier, enabling secured remote data access through a dedicated application server.

concepts.name: Audit Trail
concepts.description: XAF module that automatically tracks and persists changes to business objects, providing a complete audit history viewable in the UI and queryable in code.

concepts.name: Business Objects
concepts.description: Persistent .NET classes that define the application's data model in XAF. Business objects map to database tables via XPO or EF Core and carry validation, business logic, and security rules.

concepts.name: Non-Persistent Objects
concepts.description: Business objects that exist only in memory and are not saved to a database. Used to create views from external data sources, aggregated data, or transient UI state.

concepts.name: Business Object Lifecycle
concepts.description: Events and hooks in the lifecycle of a persistent business object: creation, property change notification, validation, saving, and deletion.

synonyms: Custom Logic Within a Data Model

concepts.name: Calculated and Aliased Properties
concepts.description: Properties whose values are computed from other data using XPO PersistentAlias, CalculatedPersistentAlias, or EF Core computed columns. Includes custom metadata and DisplayName attributes.

concepts.name: Validation
concepts.description: XAF Validation Module that enforces business rules on persistent objects through declarative validation rules, custom rule implementations, and rule sets.

concepts.name: Custom Validation Rules
concepts.description: Implementation of custom validation rule types and RuleSet customizations that extend the built-in XAF Validation Module.

concepts.name: State Machine
concepts.description: XAF State Machine Module that implements workflow-style state transitions on business objects, with configurable states, transitions, and conditional enable/hide logic.

concepts.name: Clone Object
concepts.description: XAF Clone Object Module that provides a built-in action to duplicate business objects, with support for customizing which properties and associations are cloned.

concepts.name: Object Space
concepts.description: XAF's unit-of-work abstraction that manages object identity, tracks changes, and mediates between business objects and the underlying ORM. Central to all data operations in XAF.

concepts.name: Business Logic and Data Manipulation
concepts.description: Patterns for implementing business logic within XAF, including object creation, modification, deletion, transaction management, and using ObjectSpace in non-UI contexts.

concepts.name: Async Operations
concepts.description: Support for async/await patterns and thread-safe data access in XAF, including async controllers, async business logic, and ThreadSafeDataLayer configuration.

concepts.name: XPO
concepts.description: DevExpress eXpress Persistent Objects (XPO) — the built-in .NET ORM that maps business objects to relational databases. Supports LINQ, criteria queries, associations, and schema migrations.

concepts.name: Entity Framework Core
concepts.description: Integration of Microsoft Entity Framework Core as the ORM layer for XAF applications, enabling code-first data modeling, migrations, and LINQ queries with XAF's Object Space abstraction.

concepts.name: Object Relationships
concepts.description: Associations between business objects in XAF, including one-to-many, many-to-many, and aggregated associations. Covers XPO IList associations and EF Core navigation properties.

concepts.name: File Attachments
concepts.description: XAF File Attachments Module that enables attaching files to business objects with configurable storage backends (file system, database, custom) and UI editors.

concepts.name: Auto-Increment and Sequential Numbers
concepts.description: Generating sequential or auto-increment values for business object properties in XAF, including XPO sequence generators and database identity columns.

concepts.name: Tree Node Interface
concepts.description: Implementation of the ITreeNode interface on business objects to represent hierarchical (parent-child) data structures compatible with XAF's TreeList editor.

concepts.name: Concurrency Control
concepts.description: XAF's optimistic concurrency mechanisms that detect and resolve conflicting simultaneous edits, including merge strategies and user notifications when data has been changed by another user.

keywords: Error: Object was changed by another user

concepts.name: Database Connection and Configuration
concepts.description: Configuration of database connections in XAF applications, including connection strings, multiple simultaneous database connections, and connecting to external data sources or services.

concepts.name: Multiple Databases
concepts.description: Connecting an XAF application to multiple databases simultaneously, including split storage for security, tenant isolation, or external data integration.

concepts.name: Direct SQL and Legacy DB Mapping
concepts.description: Techniques for executing raw SQL, mapping to SQL views, using composite keys, and integrating legacy database schemas that do not conform to standard XPO/EF conventions.

synonyms: Legacy DB Mapping SQL View Composite Key

concepts.name: Data Access Modes
concepts.description: Collection source data access strategies in XAF that control how list view data is loaded from the database: DataView, Server, InstantFeedback, ServerView, and Queryable modes.

concepts.name: Database Updates and Seeding
concepts.description: Mechanisms for applying database schema changes and seeding initial data when deploying or updating an XAF application, including ModuleUpdater and DBUpdater.

concepts.name: Criteria and Query Language
concepts.description: XAF's criteria language for filtering, sorting, and querying data. Built on top of DevExpress CriteriaOperator, supports both a string syntax and a fluent API.

concepts.name: Built-In Criteria Operators
concepts.description: The set of built-in DevExpress criteria operators and functions available in XAF's filter dialogs, Model Editor, and code-based queries.

concepts.name: Custom Criteria Functions
concepts.description: Implementation of custom criteria functions that extend XAF's filter expression language, usable in criteria strings, Model Editor filters, and persistent alias expressions.

concepts.name: Deployment
concepts.description: Packaging and deploying XAF applications to production environments including Windows (ClickOnce), Linux (Docker, nginx), and cloud platforms (Azure, AWS).

concepts.name: Diagnostics and Tracing
concepts.description: Tools and techniques for diagnosing XAF application issues including the XAF Tracer, XPO Profiler, LinqPad driver, and built-in logging infrastructure.

concepts.name: Logging
concepts.description: Application logging in XAF using Microsoft.Extensions.Logging, structured logging, and XAF-specific log sources for monitoring and error tracking.

concepts.name: Testing
concepts.description: Testing XAF applications at unit and integration levels, including EasyTest UI automation, unit testing with mocked ObjectSpaces, and functional testing approaches.

concepts.name: Performance Optimization
concepts.description: Techniques for improving XAF application runtime performance, including data loading strategies, UI rendering, collection source optimization, and query tuning.

concepts.name: Memory Management
concepts.description: Controlling memory consumption in XAF applications, including object space lifetime, disposal patterns, and avoiding memory leaks in long-running processes.

concepts.name: Solution Structure and Module Design
concepts.description: Recommended XAF solution structure including module project layout, platform-specific projects, and separation of concerns across layers.

concepts.name: Template Kit and CLI
concepts.description: Visual Studio project templates and .NET CLI templates for creating new XAF application solutions, modules, and platform projects.

concepts.name: NuGet Package Management
concepts.description: Managing DevExpress XAF NuGet packages including the online feed, local packages, and resolving missing assembly references after version upgrades.

concepts.name: Model Editor
concepts.description: Visual Studio integrated tool for viewing and editing the XAF Application Model at design time. Supports custom model editors and model editor controllers for extensibility.

concepts.name: Code Analysis
concepts.description: Roslyn-based static code analyzers for XAF that detect common coding mistakes, enforce XAF patterns, and provide IDE diagnostics and code fixes.

concepts.name: Version Upgrades and Migration
concepts.description: Upgrading XAF applications between DevExpress versions, resolving breaking changes, using ProjectConverter, and migrating from legacy platforms (.NET Framework, WebForms, WCF).

concepts.name: Localization
concepts.description: Localizing XAF applications into multiple languages using satellite assemblies, the DevExpress Localization Service, model-based captions, and programmatic string overrides.

concepts.name: Programmatic Localization
concepts.description: Custom localization in code: overriding captions, messages, and strings programmatically in XAF without satellite assemblies.

concepts.name: Localization in Model Editor
concepts.description: Using the Model Editor and DevExpress Localization Service portal to manage and publish translation strings for XAF applications.

concepts.name: Accessibility
concepts.description: Accessibility compliance in XAF applications, including WCAG, Section 508, keyboard navigation, screen reader support, and Right-to-Left layout.

concepts.name: Views
concepts.description: The top-level UI concept in XAF. A view is a visual container that displays business data — either a single object (Detail View) or a collection (List View, Dashboard View). Views are generated from the Application Model.

synonyms: Views and View Items and Property Editors

concepts.name: Detail View
concepts.description: A view that displays properties of a single business object for viewing or editing. Detail Views are composed of layout groups and view items mapped from business object properties.

concepts.name: List View
concepts.description: A view that displays a collection of business objects in a grid, tree list, or other list editor. Supports sorting, filtering, grouping, inline editing, and data access mode configuration.

concepts.name: View Lifecycle and Navigation
concepts.description: Creating, showing, and closing views programmatically in XAF, including popup windows, nested views, lazy loading, and master-detail navigation.

concepts.name: Master-Detail Views
concepts.description: Combining a List View and a Detail View side-by-side or nested so that selecting a record in the list shows its details inline, without opening a separate window.

concepts.name: Dashboard View
concepts.description: A composite view that contains multiple view items of mixed types (lists, detail panels, charts, custom controls) arranged in a flexible layout.

concepts.name: View Variants
concepts.description: XAF View Variants Module that allows users to switch between multiple predefined views of the same business object collection, with per-user variant persistence.

concepts.name: Dashboards Module
concepts.description: XAF integration of the DevExpress Dashboard component, enabling drag-and-drop analytical dashboards with charts, grids, filters, and custom data sources.

concepts.name: Reports Module
concepts.description: XAF Reports Module (ReportsV2) that integrates DevExpress XtraReports for designing, storing, previewing, printing, and exporting reports from XAF applications.

synonyms: Show Preview Print Export in Code

concepts.name: Charts Module
concepts.description: XAF Chart Module that adds chart views to list views, enabling visualization of business data. Supports customization via the chart wizard and code.

concepts.name: Pivot Grid Module
concepts.description: XAF Pivot Grid Module that adds pivot table and cross-tab analysis views, allowing users to summarize and analyze business data by multiple dimensions.

concepts.name: Scheduler Module
concepts.description: XAF Scheduler Module that adds calendar and appointment scheduling views to XAF applications, supporting recurring events and custom resource/event data models.

concepts.name: Office Module
concepts.description: XAF Office Module that integrates rich text editing, spreadsheet, and PDF viewer capabilities into XAF Detail Views as property editors.

synonyms: Office Module Rich Text Spreadsheets PDF

concepts.name: Property Editors
concepts.description: UI controls in XAF Detail Views that display and edit individual business object properties. Built-in editors cover strings, numbers, dates, booleans, and references. Custom editors extend this set.

concepts.name: Standard Property Editors
concepts.description: Built-in XAF property editors for common .NET types: text, number, boolean, date/time, enum, and image. Includes formatting, display, and ImmediatePostData support.

concepts.name: Lookup Property Editors
concepts.description: Property editors for selecting referenced objects from a dropdown or popup list. Includes LookupPropertyEditor, ObjectPropertyEditor, Tag Box editors, and pop-up editors.

concepts.name: Specialized Property Editors
concepts.description: Property editors for specialized data entry: criteria/filter editors, pop-up editors, and other non-standard types that go beyond basic field editing.

concepts.name: List Editors
concepts.description: UI controls that render a List View's data — grid, tree list, chart, scheduler, and custom editors. Custom list editors allow embedding any collection control into a List View.

concepts.name: TreeList Editors
concepts.description: XAF TreeList Editors Module that provides a hierarchical tree grid list editor for displaying business objects that implement ITreeNode, with filtering and inline editing support.

concepts.name: Detail View Layout
concepts.description: Arrangement of property editors and view items in a Detail View using layout groups, tab pages, and nested containers. Includes auto-generated layouts and API-based customization.

concepts.name: Layout Runtime Customization
concepts.description: Allowing end users to rearrange, hide, and save Detail View layout changes at runtime via the Layout Editor, with per-user persistence.

concepts.name: Navigation System
concepts.description: XAF's hierarchical navigation system that organizes application views into groups and items, displayed via a navigation control (accordion, tree, or ribbon). Configurable in the Model Editor and in code.

concepts.name: Theming and Styling
concepts.description: Visual theming, skins, and CSS-based styling of XAF applications, including DevExpress skin selection, skin-aware color usage, SVG graphics, and image customization.

concepts.name: Images and SVG Graphics
concepts.description: Managing images and SVG icons in XAF applications, including custom image sources, ImageLoader customization, and diagnosing missing image issues.

concepts.name: Conditional Appearance
concepts.description: XAF Conditional Appearance Module that applies dynamic formatting rules (font, color, visibility, enabled state) to views and editors based on object property values or custom conditions.

concepts.name: Notifications
concepts.description: XAF Notifications Module that delivers scheduled alerts to users via a built-in notification center, and provides ShowMessage/Toast API for inline user feedback.

concepts.name: Inline Editing
concepts.description: Editing records directly within a List View without navigating to a separate Detail View, including batch (bulk) edit mode and in-place cell editing.

concepts.name: Validation Error Highlighting
concepts.description: Customizing how validation errors are displayed to users in XAF views, including error icons, highlighting colors, error message formatting, and custom error indicators.

concepts.name: Controllers
concepts.description: XAF controllers are the primary extension point for adding custom business logic and UI behavior. They are associated with views or windows and respond to events, managing actions and interacting with the application model.

concepts.name: Built-In Controllers
concepts.description: The set of controllers shipped with XAF that provide default application behavior: saving, deleting, refreshing, navigation, new object creation, and more.

concepts.name: Actions
concepts.description: User-interface commands in XAF that appear as buttons, menu items, or toolbar entries. Actions are defined in controllers and executed in response to user interaction.

concepts.name: Action Containers
concepts.description: UI regions that host and display actions (toolbars, ribbon bands, context menus, inline grid cells). Controls which actions appear where in the application frame.

concepts.name: Action Active and Enabled State
concepts.description: Controlling the visibility (Active) and interactability (Enabled) of XAF actions based on context, object state, security, and custom business conditions.

concepts.name: Application Templates
concepts.description: XAF frame templates (form templates) that define the visual shell of the application window — the chrome surrounding views, including toolbars, navigation, and status bars.

concepts.name: Windows and Frames
concepts.description: XAF's window and frame abstraction that hosts views and their controllers. Includes main windows, popup windows, and the Frame/Window object model.

concepts.name: XAF Web API Service
concepts.description: XAF's built-in ASP.NET Core Web API Service that generates RESTful and OData endpoints for XAF business objects, enabling headless access, mobile clients, and third-party integrations.

synonyms: XAF Data and Logic in Non-XAF Apps

concepts.name: View Items
concepts.description: Non-editor components placed in XAF Detail Views: static text, hyperlinks, web browser containers, and custom view items. Extend the Detail View beyond standard property editors.

concepts.name: Lazy Loading of View Items
concepts.description: Deferring initialization of Detail View items until the user navigates to them (e.g., tab pages), reducing load time and improving performance for complex views.

concepts.name: Security Administrative UI
concepts.description: XAF's built-in views and actions for managing users, roles, and permission assignments at runtime, including the Users, Roles, and Action Permissions management screens.

concepts.name: ORM Data Model Designer
concepts.description: The visual XPO ORM Data Model Designer (ORM Wizard) for creating business classes and defining associations in Visual Studio, generating XPO-based persistent class hierarchies.
